primary_reason: This systematic review applies geographic harmonization and prevalence pooling to synthesize heterogeneous human and animal infection surveillance evidence.
This study combines passive, active longitudinal, and nested microbiome surveillance to characterize travel-associated diarrheal disease and underreporting.
This record proposes real-time naive case-fatality trajectories as a diagnostic indicator of delayed confirmation and case underreporting.
This applied surveillance analysis integrates reproduction numbers, underreporting, testing, mortality, and intensive-care occupancy to assess epidemic response.
This study develops reproduction-number estimators for a poorly identified SEIRD model and evaluates their performance through Monte Carlo simulation.
This study applies comparative policy modelling while adjusting cross-country reporting differences.
This study applies public whole-genome data and reproducible in-silico screening to characterize temporal antimicrobial-resistance surveillance patterns.
This review concerns general adverse-drug-reaction reporting and does not provide an infectious-disease outcome or a concrete route for transferring its findings to infectious surveillance.
This systematic review pools infection-eradication and recurrence outcomes across treatment strategies while diagnosing major evidence limitations.
This single urinary-tract-infection case describes an unusual pathogen and its treatment without contributing a reusable epidemiologic or surveillance design.
This methodological review critiques bias, confounding, measurement, timing, and dependence problems in coronavirus air-pollution studies.
This evidence synthesis informatively compares national tuberculosis prevalence survey findings.
This narrative management review lacks a distinct transferable epidemiological methods contribution.
This surveillance review synthesizes adverse-event monitoring tools, underreporting challenges, and requirements for effective vaccine-safety communication.
This article advocates preserving dengue-control resources during COVID-19 but does not develop or evaluate a surveillance, analytic, or policy-evaluation method.
This single malaria case documents spontaneous splenic rupture without a transferable epidemiologic or measurement contribution.
This study develops a Bayesian SIR correction for underreported cases and examines its behavior across simulated reporting scenarios.
This study develops and simulates an SEIR extension correcting diagnostic misclassification and nonrandom testing selection, with reusable software.
This study estimates passive vaccine-safety surveillance sensitivity using external incidence denominators.
This review evaluates field validation, regulation, implementation barriers, and surveillance implications of infectious-animal-disease point-of-care tests.
This VAERS case-series description of vocal-fold immobility reports temporal associations without a reusable surveillance-adjustment or causal design.
This commentary offers broad preparedness recommendations without developing, applying, or evaluating a specific epidemiological method.
This study applies data linkage and capture-recapture estimation to surveillance underreporting.
This study applies delay-adjusted fatality ratios and Bayesian Gaussian processes to reconstruct time-varying COVID-19 case ascertainment globally.
This cross-sectional regression reports demographic and geographic associations with national COVID-19 case counts but does not identify a reusable design beyond ordinary regression.
This clinical review concerns a noninfectious inflammatory skin disorder and supplies no concrete infectious-disease transfer route.
This narrative overview summarizes rabies burden and control in Nepal without substantively evaluating or prescribing a transferable epidemiologic method.
This study develops a spatial Bayesian hierarchical framework integrating active and passive surveillance while correcting under-ascertainment bias.
This cross-sectional resident survey describes needlestick frequency, reporting, and perceived blood-borne infection risk without a reusable analytic or measurement contribution.
This study applies a standardized assessment to routine immunization data quality.
This study uses a legally mandated coroner database and excess-mortality comparisons for timely COVID-19 mortality surveillance.
This study validates vaccination-register coverage using targeted parental follow-up data.
This letter argues that racialized social and biological vulnerabilities require investigation but presents no method, diagnostic analysis, or reporting framework.
This burden study combines population surveys and sentinel surveillance to derive multipliers correcting foodborne-disease underdiagnosis and underreporting.
This comparison concerns assisted-reproduction registries and does not state a concrete route for transferring its findings to infectious-disease surveillance.
This study develops decomposable transmission surveillance metrics and estimates them with a dynamic panel model for policy monitoring.
This five-patient histoplasmosis case series highlights diagnostic recognition in transplant recipients without contributing a transferable epidemiologic method.
This case report and clinical literature review characterizes post-malaria neurologic syndrome without contributing a transferable epidemiological method.
This meta-review estimates narcolepsy occurrence without developing an infectious-disease method or establishing an explicit transferable epidemiological route.
This article derives and validates an analytic estimator for real-time probabilities supporting reliable end-of-epidemic declarations.
This dengue cost-of-illness analysis applies predictive, human-capital, and underreporting adjustments that form a transferable burden-estimation design.
This study diagnoses information loss in Mexico's sentinel surveillance by comparing reported COVID-19 counts with reproduction-number-based corrections.
This article develops a hidden Markov count model with time-varying epidemic incidence and underreporting mechanisms.
This review synthesizes multi-scale airborne transmission evidence and provides methodological guidance for surveillance and outbreak evaluations.
This broad clinical and veterinary overview summarizes fascioliasis epidemiology, diagnosis, treatment, and control without a substantive methods comparison or reusable design.
This review concerns commercial serological products and monoclonal-antibody production without a transferable epidemiological measurement method.
This article substantively explains standardized European Legionnaires' disease surveillance definitions, responsibilities, reporting methods, and response practices.
This surveillance study evaluates reporting completeness and recommends denominator-based measures for detecting congenital Zika underreporting.
This letter identifies control DNA cross-reactivity that can produce false-negative SARS-CoV-2 results and surveillance underreporting.
This study combines importation simulation and transmissibility modelling to identify locations with cryptic Zika spread.
This article develops an extended compartmental epidemic model for jointly estimating hidden infections, interventions, fatality, and forecasts.
This review applies evidence-synthesis methods to healthcare-associated infectious-disease outbreak reporting.
This longitudinal HIV analysis estimates exposure effects while accounting for time-varying confounding, competing events, and missing data.
This study develops an early-warning model linking imported dengue infections to local outbreak probabilities across time and place.
This case series primarily concerns allergy to polyethylene glycol across consumer and medical products and does not evaluate an infectious outcome or infectious-surveillance method.
This burden analysis derives and validates seroprevalence-based underreporting multipliers for infections, symptoms, hospitalizations, and deaths.
This article introduces a mixture-distribution time-series model for detecting and quantifying underreporting in infectious-disease incidence data.
This study validates hospital morbidity coding against laboratory diagnosis and demonstrates data linkage as an underreporting correction.
This review critically diagnoses regional dengue surveillance weaknesses and recommends integrated surveillance and early-warning improvements.
This analytical study diagnoses parameter non-identifiability and reporting distortions that undermine estimation from pre-peak epidemic trajectories.
This cross-sectional survey describes needlestick prevalence and reporting behavior using standard comparisons without a transferable surveillance-adjustment contribution.
The title suggests a distinction between asymptomatic bacteriuria and symptom underreporting, but the absent abstract leaves the study's methods and contribution indeterminate.
This study develops delay-adjusted measures for estimating infectious-case underreporting and positivity.
This malaria study combines facility-data auditing with spatiotemporal and meteorological analyses in a transferable surveillance assessment.
This rapid review diagnoses methodological and reporting heterogeneity across epidemic-estimation studies and proposes improved reporting items.
This preprint reports the same World Mortality Dataset, authors, excess-mortality analysis, and central findings as the subsequently published retained record PMID:34190045.
This study develops and externally validates a passive-surveillance prediction model.
This population serosurvey estimates infection prevalence, undetected-infection ratios, and fatality in an underreported urban setting.
This seroprevalence study uses response-bias mitigation, representative recruitment, adjustment, and formal bias analyses to estimate infections.
This systematic review combines evidence synthesis and occurrence mapping to characterize persistent paragonimiasis transmission foci.
This clinical review summarizes neurologic manifestations and treatment issues in acute HIV infection without a transferable epidemiologic or measurement contribution.
This study compares electronic health records with death certificates to diagnose persistent underreporting of HCV mortality.
This article develops and cross-validates a local incidence-estimation method correcting passive surveillance for diagnostic stock-outs and healthcare access.
This study develops a Bayesian model that imputes missing surveillance attributes while retaining partially complete infectious-disease records.
This study applies regression models to distinguish intervention effects from seasonality and underreporting in norovirus outbreak declines.
This psychiatric case concerns a false belief of parasitic infestation rather than an infection and offers no concrete infectious-disease transfer route.
This spatial modelling study combines regression and machine-learning algorithms to target surveillance toward environmentally suitable disease areas.
This record-linkage review quantifies tuberculosis underreporting and evaluates surveillance timeliness, concordance, accuracy, and completeness.
This seroprevalence study compares immunological evidence with vaccination histories and notifications to assess program performance and underreporting.
This mini-review advances a speculative immunological hypothesis about malaria and COVID-19 without testing it or providing a substantive epidemiologic method.
This retrospective notification review describes workers experiencing biological-material accidents but does not develop a reusable surveillance or correction method.
The study designs and implements a transferable hybrid inventory method for estimating tuberculosis undernotification under weak surveillance conditions.
The article substantively explains a transferable proportional redistribution procedure for correcting ill-defined causes in burden estimates.
The study applies event detection and seasonal time-series modelling to estimate state-level COVID-19 underreporting from respiratory surveillance data.
The representative household serosurvey corrects prevalence for sampling design and test performance while assessing infection risk patterns.
This pharmacy-sales validation study measures injection-equipment access and opioid-related needs without observing an infectious outcome or specifying an infectious-surveillance transfer.
This review concerns harmful algal blooms and toxin-related ecological and health events rather than infectious disease, with no concrete infectious transfer route.
The authors develop a state-space epidemic model that explicitly estimates time-varying undetected infections and associated transmission parameters.
This convenience-sample survey describes quarantine attitudes and self-reported preventive practices using standard cross-sectional associations without a reusable design contribution.
The study links hospital and mortality records using explicit coding rules to identify misclassified AIDS deaths and correct mortality estimates.
This clinical review addresses a noninfectious inflammatory skin condition and provides no concrete route to infectious-disease methods.
The study develops, compares, and validates machine-learning classifiers for detecting suspected foodborne outbreaks from surveillance records.
This systematic review derives denominator-based minimum infection and colonization risks from molecularly confirmed duodenoscope outbreaks.
The study directly compares susceptibility interpretation rules that alter antimicrobial-resistance classification and reporting.
The expert review identifies surveillance-system deficiencies and gives concrete recommendations for standardized surgical-site infection monitoring programs.
This computable phenotype identifies transgender and gender-nonconforming patients, while sexually transmitted infections are only a secondary observed disparity rather than the method's target.
The qualitative study uses stakeholder interviews to examine implementation barriers and unintended effects of infectious-disease control policies in a camp.
The integrated human and mosquito investigation demonstrates an active-surveillance design for detecting unreported co-circulating arboviruses.
The prospective community cohort demonstrates active febrile surveillance with multimodal confirmation for estimating symptomatic dengue incidence.
The study develops a method for incorporating diagnostic false negatives into an epidemic model and validates projections against serosurveys.
This three-case series and clinical review describes varicella-zoster meningitis without a reusable epidemiologic, diagnostic-validation, or surveillance design.
This paper describes elevated COVID-19 mortality among African political leaders and discusses possible explanations without establishing a transferable analytic design.
The analysis combines direct and excess-mortality estimators with testing adjustment and penalized prediction to quantify COVID-19 death underreporting.
The ecological study uses quasi-Poisson generalized additive models to estimate expected respiratory mortality and detect heterogeneous pandemic excesses.
The study performs concordance and underreporting diagnostics across four immunization-financing reporting systems, identifying specific measurement inconsistencies.
The systematic review synthesizes evidence on sexually transmitted infections and related reproductive-health outcomes in an underreported population.
This review summarizes reptilian herpesvirus taxonomy, pathology, and epidemiology without substantively comparing or developing surveillance or analytic methods.
The study compares specialist-reviewed clinical records with registry reports to quantify device-infection underreporting and surveillance deficiencies.
The meta-analysis quantifies systematic under-reporting in a widely used HIV surveillance measurement.
The article develops an integrated statistical framework for identifying epidemic cycles, underreporting effects, onset dates, and likely endpoints.
The study uses operational surveillance data and policy timing to assess how funding and testing shifts altered COVID-19 case ascertainment.
The article audits race and ethnicity reporting in COVID-19 studies and identifies systematic omissions affecting evidence interpretation.
The title concerns reporting of genetic variants causing a noninfectious syndrome and supplies no concrete infectious-disease transfer route.
The review provides testing, follow-up, and management guidance for HIV-syphilis coinfection while identifying unresolved diagnostic challenges.
The cross-sectional serosurvey applies antibody testing to quantify under-ascertained SARS-CoV-2 burden and characterize time-dependent serologic responses.
The study benchmarks five published epidemic models against held-out Indian surveillance data while comparing forecasts, uncertainty, and underreporting estimates.
This retrospective malaria report presents counts, maps, ordinary correlations, and spatial clustering among miners without a reusable measurement or causal-design contribution.
The serosurvey compares vaccinated and unvaccinated infants and models factors associated with measles and rubella seropositivity.
The registered systematic review and meta-analysis synthesize regional prevalence evidence for vulvovaginal candidiasis and identify surveillance gaps.
The hospital-based enhanced surveillance design estimates infant pertussis incidence and clinical burden beyond routine notification systems.
The authors develop a mechanistic epidemic model with underreporting observation and policy mechanisms, then conduct counterfactual intervention simulations.
The analysis integrates phylogenetics, travel data, excess mortality, and an SEIR model to reconstruct and forecast Iran's epidemic.
This study develops a quantitative transmission-risk estimator incorporating underreporting and asymptomatic-infection corrections.
This study applies and compares mixed negative-binomial models to characterize heterogeneous norovirus surveillance reporting.
This computational study evaluates how observation-function misspecification affects epidemic filtering and forecasting estimates.
The openly maintained mortality dataset enables reproducible excess-mortality estimation and diagnosis of pandemic death under-reporting.

This report compares notified tuberculosis and influenza counts before and during COVID-19 but cannot distinguish underreporting, misclassification, and true incidence changes through a reusable design.
This structured review describes the clinical characteristics of 83 pediatric histoplasmosis cases without a transferable epidemiologic or surveillance method.
This applied Monte Carlo analysis quantifies age-specific vaccination benefits and adverse-event risks for policy decisions.
This study develops a transferable two-strain transmission framework for estimating mutant-strain takeover times.
This study combines multisource surveillance, spatial analysis, and mixed modelling to investigate interspecies rabies dynamics.
This mechanistic behavioral model estimates underreporting, adherence fatigue, and intervention scenarios across multiple countries.
This preprint represents the same Sierra Leone serosurvey subsequently retained as the journal publication.
This compartmental modelling application estimates infection and death underreporting across two epidemic waves.
This mixed observational design uses presentation trends and a community survey to assess post-epidemic healthcare seeking.
This article develops a simple age-structured method for early estimation of unreported epidemic infections.
This validation compares international opioid-availability statistics with Scandinavian sales data and does not provide an infectious outcome or concrete infectious-surveillance transfer route.
This study describes tick-borne encephalitis notification trends before and during COVID-19 but offers only speculative explanations for the observed decline.
This article provides substantive guidance on interpreting spontaneous safety reports and supporting vaccine-signal detection.
This metapopulation transmission model evaluates how mobility, migration, interventions, and fuel shortages shaped epidemic spread.
This decision-tree model evaluates dengue vaccination and screening through cost-effectiveness and budget-impact analyses.
Synthetic patient generation supports an association analysis but does not evaluate or develop an eligible epidemiological estimator.
This narrative review summarizes regional meningococcal epidemiology without a transferable methodological contribution.
This living systematic review applies risk-of-bias and evidence-certainty methods to evolving COVID-19 treatment evidence.
This systematic review and random-effects meta-analysis estimates severe yellow-fever case fatality risk across studies.
This mechanistic epidemic analysis derives how transmission heterogeneity and underreporting affect elimination-time inference.
The study supplies an explicit correction factor for surveillance coverage and cause-of-death certification errors.
This review concerns medication-induced constipation and provides no infectious outcome or concrete route to infectious-disease methodology.
This study applies a Bayesian hierarchical model to estimate spatially varying leprosy underreporting from disability indicators.
The survey describes pediatrician knowledge and practices without developing or evaluating a transferable epidemiological method.
